parameters-en.Drive: Controls the amount of gain applied to the signal, which influences the level of distortion.
parameters-en.Bass: Controls the low frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Variamp: This 5-position switch acts selects the mid frequency to be boosted or cut with the Effect parameter.
parameters-en.Effect: Controls the mid EQ of the tonestack, the frequency of which is controlled by the Variamp parameter.
parameters-en.Treble: Controls the high frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Level: Level controls the overall level of the Preamp block. Unlike Master Volume, it has no effect on the block's tone. Level is a great way to level your presets as you can quickly jump to each Amp block by pressing the Amp button.
parameters-en.Master: Controls the Master Volume of the amplifier.
parameters-en.Bright: When on, boosts high frequencies. Is more obvious when Drive is set low.
parameters-en.Hum: Adjusts the level of power supply hum that interacts with the signal. Preamp tube heaters in tube amps will leak a little bit of 60 cycle hum into the audio signal. When this hum mixes with the distorted audio signal, a non-musical distortion is created at low levels. To some players, this low-level, non-harmonic distortion adds a bit of realism to the amp model.
parameters-en.Tuning Fork: Controls the volume of the Tuning Fork's reference tone. Included only in the interest of hyper-accuracy.
parameters-en.TF Coarse: Controls the coarse tuning of the Tuning Fork's reference tone.
parameters-en.TF Fine: Controls the fine tuning of the Tuning Fork's reference tone.
